# Work Packages: Shared Statechart AST

**Inputs**: Design documents from `/kitty-specs/020-shared-statechart-ast/`
**Prerequisites**: plan.md (required), spec.md (user stories), research.md, data-model.md
**Tests**: Tests are included (spec explicitly requires test migration and new AST tests).

**Organization**: Fine-grained subtasks (`Txxx`) roll up into work packages (`WPxx`). Each work package must be independently deliverable and testable.

**Prompt Files**: Each work package references a matching prompt file in `/tasks/` generated by `/spec-kitty.tasks`. Treat this file as the high-level checklist; keep deep implementation detail inside the prompt files.

## Subtask Format: `[Txxx] [P?] Description`
- **[P]** indicates the subtask can proceed in parallel (different files/components).
- Include precise file paths or modules.

## Path Conventions
- **Source**: `src/Frank.Statecharts/`
- **Tests**: `test/Frank.Statecharts.Tests/`

---

## Work Package WP01: Shared AST Type Definitions (Priority: P0)

**Goal**: Define all shared AST types in a new `Ast/Types.fs` file and update the project compile order. This is the foundational type library that all subsequent work depends on.
**Independent Test**: `dotnet build src/Frank.Statecharts/Frank.Statecharts.fsproj` succeeds across all three target frameworks (net8.0/net9.0/net10.0) with the new types compiled.
**Prompt**: `/tasks/WP01-shared-ast-type-definitions.md`
**Estimated Prompt Size**: ~500 lines

### Included Subtasks
- [ ] T001 Create `src/Frank.Statecharts/Ast/Types.fs` with namespace `Frank.Statecharts.Ast` and define `SourcePosition` struct, `HistoryKind` DU, `StateKind` DU (9 cases), `ArrowStyle`/`Direction`/`TransitionStyle`/`WsdNotePosition`/`WsdMeta` types, `AlpsTransitionKind`/`AlpsMeta` stub, `ScxmlMeta` stub, `SmcatMeta` stub, `XStateMeta` stub, `Annotation` DU (5 cases)
- [ ] T002 Define `StateActivities` record, `DataEntry` record, `NoteContent` record, `GroupKind` DU (7 cases), `Directive` DU in `Ast/Types.fs`
- [ ] T003 Define mutually recursive types: `GroupBranch`, `GroupBlock`, `StateNode`, `TransitionEdge`, `StatechartElement` DU (5 cases), `StatechartDocument` record using F# `and` keyword
- [ ] T004 Define `ParseFailure` record (with `Position: SourcePosition option`), `ParseWarning` record (with `Position: SourcePosition option`), `ParseResult` record (with `Document: StatechartDocument`) in `Ast/Types.fs`
- [ ] T005 Update `Frank.Statecharts.fsproj` to add `<Compile Include="Ast/Types.fs" />` as the FIRST compile item (before `Wsd/Types.fs`)
- [ ] T006 [P] Verify multi-target build: `dotnet build` succeeds for net8.0, net9.0, and net10.0

### Implementation Notes
- All types must be `public` (not `internal`) per PD-001
- Mutually recursive types (`StatechartElement`, `GroupBranch`, `GroupBlock`, `StateNode`) require F# `and` keyword (PD-006)
- `SourcePosition` must be `[<Struct>]` for performance
- All record types must support structural equality (no mutable fields)
- `ParseFailure.Position` and `ParseWarning.Position` become `SourcePosition option` (generalized from non-optional in WSD)

### Parallel Opportunities
- T006 (build verification) can run after T001-T005 are complete.

### Dependencies
- None (starting package).

### Risks & Mitigations
- **Type ordering**: F# requires types to be defined before use. The mutually recursive types need careful ordering with `type ... and ...` blocks.
- **Namespace collision**: The shared `GroupKind`, `SourcePosition` etc. may conflict with WSD types if both are `open`-ed. Mitigation: WSD types will be migrated in WP02.

---

## Work Package WP02: Migrate WSD Types & Lexer (Priority: P0)

**Goal**: Remove types that moved to the shared AST from `Wsd/Types.fs` (keeping only lexer-specific types: `TokenKind`, `Token`) and update `Wsd/Lexer.fs` to import `SourcePosition` from the shared AST namespace.
**Independent Test**: `dotnet build src/Frank.Statecharts/Frank.Statecharts.fsproj` succeeds. The lexer still produces tokens with correct `SourcePosition` values.
**Prompt**: `/tasks/WP02-migrate-wsd-types-and-lexer.md`
**Estimated Prompt Size**: ~350 lines

### Included Subtasks
- [ ] T007 Modify `src/Frank.Statecharts/Wsd/Types.fs`: remove `SourcePosition`, `ArrowStyle`, `Direction`, `Participant`, `Message`, `GuardAnnotation`, `NotePosition`, `Note`, `GroupKind`, `GroupBranch`, `Group`, `DiagramElement`, `Diagram`, `ParseFailure`, `ParseWarning`, `ParseResult`. Keep only `TokenKind` and `Token` (with `Token.Position` now referencing `Frank.Statecharts.Ast.SourcePosition`)
- [ ] T008 Update `src/Frank.Statecharts/Wsd/Types.fs` module declaration: add `open Frank.Statecharts.Ast` and change `Token.Position` type to use shared `SourcePosition`
- [ ] T009 Update `src/Frank.Statecharts/Wsd/Lexer.fs`: change `open Frank.Statecharts.Wsd.Types` to also `open Frank.Statecharts.Ast` for `SourcePosition` access
- [ ] T010 Verify build succeeds with no remaining references to removed types from `Wsd.Types`

### Implementation Notes
- The `Wsd/Types.fs` module should remain `module internal Frank.Statecharts.Wsd.Types` since `TokenKind` and `Token` are WSD-internal concepts
- `Token` struct must import `SourcePosition` from `Frank.Statecharts.Ast` (D-009)
- The lexer file (`Lexer.fs`) only creates `Token` values with `SourcePosition`, so minimal changes needed

### Parallel Opportunities
- None within this WP (sequential dependency on type removal).

### Dependencies
- Depends on WP01 (shared AST types must exist before WSD types can reference them).

### Risks & Mitigations
- **Compilation cascade**: Removing types from `Wsd.Types` will break `GuardParser.fs` and `Parser.fs`. Those are fixed in WP03. Build will not succeed until WP03 is also complete. Mitigation: WP02 and WP03 are sequential and should be implemented together.

---

## Work Package WP03: Migrate WSD Parser & GuardParser (Priority: P0)

**Goal**: Migrate `GuardParser.fs` and `Parser.fs` to produce shared AST types (`StatechartDocument`, `StateNode`, `TransitionEdge`, etc.) instead of WSD-specific types (`Diagram`, `Participant`, `Message`, etc.).
**Independent Test**: `dotnet build src/Frank.Statecharts/Frank.Statecharts.fsproj` succeeds. The parser's `parseWsd` function returns a `ParseResult` containing a `StatechartDocument`.
**Prompt**: `/tasks/WP03-migrate-wsd-parser.md`
**Estimated Prompt Size**: ~600 lines

### Included Subtasks
- [ ] T011 Migrate `src/Frank.Statecharts/Wsd/GuardParser.fs`: change return type to use shared `ParseFailure`/`ParseWarning`/`GuardAnnotation` from `Ast.Types`. The `GuardAnnotation` type stays in WSD as a parse helper but uses shared `SourcePosition`
- [ ] T012 Migrate `src/Frank.Statecharts/Wsd/Parser.fs` `ParserState`: change `Participants` map value type, `Elements` to `StatechartElement list`, `Errors` to shared `ParseFailure list`, `Warnings` to shared `ParseWarning list`
- [ ] T013 Migrate parser `parseParticipant`: produce `StateDecl of StateNode` instead of `ParticipantDecl of Participant`. Map `Name` -> `Identifier`, `Alias` -> `Label`, set `Kind = Regular`, `Explicit` tracking stays in `ParserState`
- [ ] T014 Migrate parser `parseMessage`: produce `TransitionElement of TransitionEdge` instead of `MessageElement of Message`. Map `Sender`/`Receiver` -> `Source`/`Target`, `ArrowStyle`/`Direction` -> `WsdAnnotation of WsdTransitionStyle`, `Label` -> `Event`, `Parameters` -> `Parameters`
- [ ] T015 Migrate parser `parseNote`, `parseGroup`, `parseTitleDirective`, `parseAutoNumberDirective`, and `parse`/`parseWsd` top-level functions. Map `NoteElement of Note` -> `NoteElement of NoteContent`, `GroupElement of Group` -> `GroupElement of GroupBlock`, `TitleDirective` -> `DirectiveElement of TitleDirective`, `AutoNumberDirective` -> `DirectiveElement of AutoNumberDirective`, `Diagram` -> `StatechartDocument`
- [ ] T016 Update `addError`/`addWarning` helpers: `ParseFailure.Position` becomes `Some pos` (wrap in option). `ParseWarning.Position` becomes `Some pos`. Ensure error-limit sentinel also wraps position

### Implementation Notes
- `ParserState` needs an internal `ExplicitParticipants: Set<string>` or similar to track which participants were explicitly declared (the `Explicit` field was on `Participant` which is now `StateNode` and has no `Explicit` concept)
- `ArrowStyle`/`Direction` on messages become `WsdAnnotation(WsdTransitionStyle { ArrowStyle = ...; Direction = ... })` in the `TransitionEdge.Annotations` list
- `NotePosition` on notes becomes `WsdAnnotation(WsdNotePosition ...)` in the `NoteContent.Annotations` list
- `Guard` on notes: the guard content should flow to the `NoteContent` annotations or remain as note content (the guard itself populates the `TransitionEdge.Guard` field in downstream processing, not at parse time)
- `Diagram.AutoNumber` becomes a `DirectiveElement(AutoNumberDirective ...)` element already emitted; `StatechartDocument` does not have an `AutoNumber` field
- `Diagram.Participants` list is derived from `StateDecl` elements; `StatechartDocument` does not have a separate `Participants` field

### Parallel Opportunities
- T011 (GuardParser) can proceed in parallel with T012-T016 (Parser) since they modify different files, but T012-T016 are sequential within Parser.fs.

### Dependencies
- Depends on WP02 (WSD types must be stripped down first).

### Risks & Mitigations
- **Behavioral regression**: The parser's error recovery, implicit participant tracking, and group nesting logic must be preserved exactly. Mitigation: Existing test suite (migrated in WP04) will catch regressions.
- **GuardAnnotation**: The `GuardAnnotation` type in WSD is a parse helper. It should remain as an internal type in the WSD parser or be redefined locally. The shared AST does not have a `GuardAnnotation` type -- guard data flows to `TransitionEdge.Guard` or `NoteContent.Annotations`.

---

## Work Package WP04: Migrate WSD Test Files (Priority: P1)

**Goal**: Update all WSD parser test files to assert against shared AST types instead of WSD-specific types. All existing tests must pass with semantically equivalent assertions.
**Independent Test**: `dotnet test test/Frank.Statecharts.Tests/` passes with all WSD tests green.
**Prompt**: `/tasks/WP04-migrate-wsd-tests.md`
**Estimated Prompt Size**: ~550 lines

### Included Subtasks
- [ ] T017 Migrate `test/Frank.Statecharts.Tests/Wsd/GuardParserTests.fs`: change `open Frank.Statecharts.Wsd.Types` to `open Frank.Statecharts.Ast`, update `SourcePosition` construction, update `GuardAnnotation` assertions
- [ ] T018 Migrate `test/Frank.Statecharts.Tests/Wsd/ParserTests.fs`: change helper functions (`messages`, `notes`, `participantDecls`) to use `StatechartElement` DU cases (`TransitionElement`, `NoteElement`, `StateDecl`). Update all assertions: `Diagram` -> `Document`, `Participants` -> derived from elements, `Participant.Name` -> `StateNode.Identifier`, `Message.Sender/Receiver` -> `TransitionEdge.Source/Target`, `Message.ArrowStyle/Direction` -> extracted from `TransitionEdge.Annotations`
- [ ] T019 Migrate `test/Frank.Statecharts.Tests/Wsd/GroupingTests.fs`: update `GroupElement of Group` -> `GroupElement of GroupBlock`, `GroupBranch` -> shared `GroupBranch`, `GroupKind` -> shared `GroupKind`, update message/note extraction within branches
- [ ] T020 Migrate `test/Frank.Statecharts.Tests/Wsd/ErrorTests.fs`: update `ParseFailure.Position` assertions (now `SourcePosition option`), update element extraction helpers, update `DiagramElement` -> `StatechartElement` pattern matching
- [ ] T021 Migrate `test/Frank.Statecharts.Tests/Wsd/RoundTripTests.fs`: update onboarding and tic-tac-toe tests. `Diagram` -> `Document`, `Participants` -> derived, `Message.ArrowStyle/Direction` -> annotation extraction, `Note.Guard` -> annotation or updated field, `Note.NotePosition` -> annotation extraction
- [ ] T022 Update `test/Frank.Statecharts.Tests/Frank.Statecharts.Tests.fsproj`: add compile entries for new test files (WP05) if needed. Update Wsd test file paths if any moved

### Implementation Notes
- Each test file needs helper functions updated to extract typed data from shared AST types
- Arrow style assertions: Instead of `msg.ArrowStyle = Solid`, need to extract from `msg.Annotations` list, e.g., find `WsdAnnotation(WsdTransitionStyle ts)` and check `ts.ArrowStyle`
- Note position assertions: Instead of `note.NotePosition = Over`, need to extract from `note.Annotations`
- Participant assertions: Instead of `p.Name` and `p.Explicit`, use `StateNode.Identifier` and check against parser's internal tracking (or check warnings)
- `result.Diagram.Title` becomes `result.Document.Title`
- `result.Diagram.AutoNumber` becomes: check for `DirectiveElement(AutoNumberDirective _)` in `result.Document.Elements`
- `result.Diagram.Participants` becomes: filter `result.Document.Elements` for `StateDecl` nodes

### Parallel Opportunities
- T017 (GuardParserTests) can proceed in parallel with T018-T021 since it modifies a different file.
- T018-T021 modify different test files and could theoretically proceed in parallel, but share helper function patterns.

### Dependencies
- Depends on WP03 (parser must produce shared AST types before tests can assert against them).

### Risks & Mitigations
- **Test count regression**: Every existing test must continue to pass. Mitigation: Run `dotnet test` after each file migration to catch issues incrementally.
- **Assertion depth**: Arrow style/note position extraction from annotations adds verbosity to tests. Mitigation: Create shared helper functions for extracting WSD-specific annotations.

---

## Work Package WP05: New Shared AST Tests (Priority: P1)

**Goal**: Add new test files validating shared AST type construction, partial population by format parsers, structural equality, and source position tracking. Covers User Stories 1, 3, 4, and 5.
**Independent Test**: `dotnet test test/Frank.Statecharts.Tests/` passes with all new AST tests green.
**Prompt**: `/tasks/WP05-new-shared-ast-tests.md`
**Estimated Prompt Size**: ~500 lines

### Included Subtasks
- [ ] T023 [P] Create `test/Frank.Statecharts.Tests/Ast/TypeConstructionTests.fs`: test programmatic construction of `StatechartDocument` (tic-tac-toe example with 4 states, transitions with guards, initial state marker), verify all fields populate, verify structural equality by constructing two identical ASTs independently
- [ ] T024 [P] Create `test/Frank.Statecharts.Tests/Ast/PartialPopulationTests.fs`: construct ASTs as if from each of the 5 format parsers (WSD, ALPS, SCXML, smcat, XState) using only fields that format can express. Verify unpopulated fields are `None` or `[]`. Covers US3 scenarios
- [ ] T025 [P] Add annotation coexistence tests: construct AST nodes with WSD annotations (arrow style) and SCXML annotations (invoke, history) on the same node. Verify both can be attached and retrieved independently. Covers US5 scenarios
- [ ] T026 [P] Add source position tests: verify `SourcePosition` `None` for programmatic construction, `Some` for parser output. Parse a WSD text and verify AST nodes carry correct line/column values
- [ ] T027 [P] Add edge case tests: empty document, state with no transitions, transition with no event, self-transition, multiple transitions same source/target, state with `IsInitial` and `IsFinal` (warning), deeply nested hierarchy, empty data entry expression, empty annotations list, unicode identifiers
- [ ] T028 Update `test/Frank.Statecharts.Tests/Frank.Statecharts.Tests.fsproj`: add `<Compile Include="Ast/TypeConstructionTests.fs" />` and `<Compile Include="Ast/PartialPopulationTests.fs" />` to compile order

### Implementation Notes
- New test files go in `test/Frank.Statecharts.Tests/Ast/` directory (mirror source structure)
- Tests use Expecto framework (matching existing test patterns)
- Structural equality tests: `Expect.equal ast1 ast2 "identical ASTs are equal"`
- Partial population tests construct `StatechartDocument` values manually (not via parser) to simulate each format
- The tic-tac-toe construction test from US1 should build a full game model

### Parallel Opportunities
- All subtasks (T023-T027) modify different test files and can proceed in parallel.

### Dependencies
- Depends on WP01 (shared AST types must exist).
- Can proceed in parallel with WP02/WP03/WP04 since these tests construct ASTs programmatically.

### Risks & Mitigations
- **Test file count**: Adding 2 new test files requires updating the `.fsproj` compile order. Mitigation: T028 covers this.

---

## Dependency & Execution Summary

- **Sequence**: WP01 -> WP02 -> WP03 -> WP04 (critical path for parser migration)
- **Parallel**: WP05 can start after WP01, running in parallel with WP02/WP03
- **MVP Scope**: WP01 + WP02 + WP03 + WP04 (shared types defined, parser migrated, tests passing)
- **Full Scope**: All 5 work packages (adds comprehensive new AST validation tests)

### Execution Diagram

```
WP01 (AST Types)
  |
  +---> WP02 (WSD Types/Lexer) ---> WP03 (Parser Migration) ---> WP04 (Test Migration)
  |
  +---> WP05 (New AST Tests) [parallel with WP02-WP04]
```

---
